text: Unfortunately I am rating a 1 star. We went around 5pm in the evening , no one else was there so when we went to order , the lady who was helping us was already annoyed and rushing us. i first ordered hot chocolate and she abruptly noted that she wanted us to order deserts before the drinks. We said , ok.  We asked questions about some of the deserts and she was very dismissive and again , rushing us. After ordering , we got about 8 things, she rings us up , wraps things up and went on to someone who had walked in . Still no drink. We say , you forgot about our drink , she's looks at us , super annoyed and confused ,  then I repeated to her what she initially told us and she said , oh yea. She makes the drink for Us then says ,' it's on me ' , but it was clearly on the bill. I have absolutely no interest in going back. And the goods were 'so-so' most definitely not worth the cost. I don't mind paying for quality but this  wasn't quality all around. Horrible customer service

text: My fiancé went there Saturday night for his bachelor party. While there, he was playing the table games and drinking a few beers but not showing signs of over served in the least. He and one of his buddies went to the bar where his buddy bought them both a drink. The female bartender served my fiancé's friend, but not my fiancé. When he asked where his beer was, she said its coming then walked away. After waiting at the bar for about 10 minutes without getting his drink, I approached him at the bar asking what's taking so long. He reiterated what the bartender told him earlier that is was coming soon. He kept having to wave his hands to get the bartender's attention and she would just look at him and help someone else. He then asked the male bartender what's going on? He ignored him as the female bartender was explaining the situation. He approached us and said the bar staff got word to not sell my fiancé anymore alcohol and to talk to the manager for further info. Then a man in a suit walked up to us, asked for my fiancé's ID, wrote out his license number and explained that they are refusing to sell him alcohol. But he did not have a reason why. My fiancé has a Myotonic Muscular Dystrophy so he already stumbles and slurs when he is stone-cold sober. He only had at most 3-4 beers in the course of 3 hours. So he was not by any means, overly intoxicated. We explained the situation to the manger that he has a disease that makes him that way. He just looked at all of us and acted like his disease meant nothing. That my fiancé was over his drinking limit. All he wanted was a nice night out with his friends and family to celebrate our upcoming wedding! This is discrimination! We will be filing a formal complaint!
label: negative

text: It was paacckkkeeddd when we arrived so I knew we were in for a treat. Were shopping in the area and found this place searching for mimosas on Yelp. Service was great. Our server paid special attention and worked to meet our requests (bcus I usually have several questions and requests). Lavender lemonade fireeeeee. My sister loved her megamosa and mom loved her honey margarita. Ordered the crispy brussels for a starter. So good and their homemade ranch 10/10. Our food was soooo good. I had to get the grits with goat cheese and glad I did. The bacon was cooked perfectly. Usually dont love eggs but theirs were so good added cheese. 10/10!!!!!!!! I added a fried chicken breast with some they spicy sauce and more ranch. The whole meal was excellent. I tried my sister pancakes and also fire. Finally dessert.... be sure to check yelp .... but the pecan was pie was great shouldve requested no chocolate drizzle but I know for next time & the banana pudding was good but too watery for my liking. All in all if you thinking about going here. Go. Thanks

text: I would give 0 stars if I could. I made a mobile order. I decided to go into the store and check on my order after waiting 12 minutes in the designated parking spot. The cashier looks in the system and said "We don't have your order". I pull up the app and confirm that I made an order, showed my receipt to the cashier. She gets a manager, Karina. The manager looks in the computer and says "we don't have your order". I preceded to show her my order on the mobile app. She walks away. I wait thinking she's fulfilling my order. Nope, she just walked away. So ask the cashier to get the manager back. The manager proceeds to tell me there is nothing they can do if the order isn't in their system. She can't be sure via the mobile app that I paid. I proceed to show her the email confirmation which has my credit card information, the authorization code and amount paid. She continues to say there is nothing she can do. Mobile ordering is different from McDonald's. Even though it was the McDonald's app that I used! When money is taken and services or products are not received that is by definition STEALING! I'm currently waiting on corporate to refund my monies. I will never go to this establishment again.

text: Sadly, I was really disappointed with my experience at Coffeebar this weekend. Still being relatively new to town, I've been searching highest rated ... fill in the blank, and trying to experience all Reno has to offer. Coffeebar popped up as very highly rated, so my boyfriend and I went there this weekend. First off, we showed up at 11, which was our mistake. If you're looking for assorted baked goods, definitely get there before 11 or you will be left to choose between a chocolate or plain croissant. I will say the plain croissant was pretty good though. We were somewhat bummed because my BF loves flavored coffees and I thought this place would definitely have them, but upon arrival we didn't see any listed and it was fairly busy, so we tried to keep it moving. I was also excited to try a lavender brown sugar latte I saw someone mention below, but didn't locate it on the menu, so I got my usual chai tea. Now, I love chai tea and I don't think I'm a snob. Before this weekend I had never met a chai tea latte I didn't like. Honestly, the Coffeebar chai tea latte was the worst I've ever had. I guess they were out of agave so they substituted vanilla, which was more than fine with me. The chai tasted like someone had made a very weak cup of chai tea, added half a shot of milk, and called it a day. No frothing, just unceremoniously dumped. It was super watery and there was absolutely no vanilla taste at all. For that matter, it was the furthest from a creamy latte I expected. My BF ordered plain coffee, but didn't find it to taste very good, so he actually made some coffee when we returned home instead. We also ordered 2 breakfast sandwiches which were pretty lackluster and ended up in the garbage. All in all it was a disappointing outing and I don't think we'll be back.
label: negative

text: This with no exaggeration was the worst restaurant experience I have ever had.  From start to finish!  The employees should not be working here and honestly should never be in hospitality.  We arrive at 7pm on 1/15/22 for a party of 8.  They told us it would be 45 minute wait.  We were ok because we knew it was busy because of a huge event at the convention this weekend.  After about an hour we went make to front desk and they said maybe another 10 minutes.  20 minutes later they said their kitchen was backed up and they were all going to take their breaks first...ok?  These girls were the most unprofessional workers ever.  Drinking, swearing and having an 'I don't care attitude'.  Finally it's about 8:30'and they said since their kitchen is backed up and we had kids with us they will not seat us!!!!  Talk about discrimination.  Our kids were starving and we waited over an hour and half!!!!  We would have left but everything else in the area was closing or booked that late.  We finally get the manager and she is the nicest person in the world.  Gets us a table and says will rush.  Server was nice and a sweatheart.  But it doesn't stop here.  10:15 all our food comes in a to go bag from the kitchen manager and said we had to leave because minors not allowed.  Both the server and manager of the location were in shock and they said they were embarrassed.  I just wanted to get the heck out of there.  I have never felt so disrespected in my entire life.  I get that it turns into a bar after 10 but they made my kids and family feel like absolute shit.  Manager was nice but needs to own it.  Her server was great and I still tipped her 25%.  The people at the front desk should not be in the service, hospitality business or anything that is client facing.  They were horrible and honestly the most unprofessional people I've seen.  Horrible and the WORST experience ever!   I hope everyone reads this and make sure they know what happens when you let crappy people be the face of your company!  Our kids ate dinner in a Uber car after waiting for 4 plus hours!  Shame on you!!!
label: negative

text:
I've been a customer at the same
Location for over 20 years. (I think it has changed management more than once). The quality of there muffins has gone down so badly. Their Banana Nut was my absolute fave. It was the bomb. It tasted like my mom's homemade banana bread - super moist, tons of banana flavor, and plenty of nuts. I also use to enjoy the Carrot Cake muffin. It also tasted homemade. 
I've had both kinds of muffin recently and they were awful. So bad that my husband forgot I didn't like them anymore and he brought me one on Saturday and I refused to eat it. I just couldn't force myself to do it.

I don't know if the muffin mix comes from corporate like this awful concoction or if it's the franchisee cutting corners to save money, but either way both muffins are a horrible representation of this company. 

And one other note - the customer service has also gone downhill. I get that on the weekends, it's probably high school kids working that don't want to be there but a company needs to know who they put into positions as their representation of their company. The staff always acts completely put out by customers ordering food. They take no pride in their work. Again, this is just to let the owner know how their employees reflect on their business. Poorly, they reflect poorly....as do the muffins they make.

text:
Is someone pulling your leg?  Denny's is a step above Waffle House which isn't saying much. I Hop has gone downhill but a step or two above Denny's which is not saying much.

The only good place to get breakfast in Santa Barbara is Jeanine's.  

Best  in SBC is Ellen's Pancake House in Buellton about a 35 minute gorgeous ocean and scenic drive north up 101 just off the freeway.  

They are the best of the best in SBC.  Quality and amount of food is huge... prices fair.  Expect a line of people waiting to get a table or counter seat that are locals not tourists.  Prepare to go out for a walk or hike.  One doesn't go away hungry. Staff are fantastic and many have worked there for years.  Exceptional service. Unlike most breakfast lunch restaurants family owned and run onsite and cooks and servers that have worked there for decades.

Several of our favorites are chicken fried steak and eggs with thick apple cured bacon bits added to gravy with homemade biscuits and gravy, NY steak and eggs, thick ham steak and eggs, 3, 6 or 12 egg omelettes made any way one likes.  Never, ever forget the thin Danish pancakes that can be ordered with fruit.  They are truly a delicacy and come with powdered sugar dusting.  Just a little butter and real maple syrup is all they need.  If one wants them a tad sweeter the preserves they offer will put it over the top.  Prepare for a to go box.  Local ranchers and farmers eat here for breakfast starting at about 6am as they need a hearty breakfast to make it to lunch or dinner.
label: negative

text:
I used to be a regular at blueprint Delmar, a daily patron and would even attend their special events excitedly. However, at one of their after hours coffee tasting events I was targeted and harassed by a worker who was sexist, believe it or not! 

I sat through the event with a woman friend of mine, and when I went to use the restroom, one of their workers who is also a woman decided to whisper to my friend that she should be wary of me as a person and that she didn't trust me. For some context I had had ZERO negative experiences here prior and had spent many many hours there enjoying time with both men and women friends... so for their worker to had the audacity to impose on my social circuit with their unwarranted suspicion was absolutely astounding to me.

My friend told me that this happened as soon as we left the event. She was very uncomfortable with the fact that the worker chose to say that and quickly reassured me that she didn't have any concerns about my integrity, thank goodness.

So at this point my heart is racing, like borderline anxiety attack, and I'm absolutely confused about why I was targeted and why this had happened after I had spent so much time there, even time ordering from and small talking with this exact worker! I decide to go back in in order to get some clarification and correct the situation, hoping to put my anxiety to rest and also offer some kind of reassurance of my integrity to the person who was suspicious of me.

I calmly walked up to her and said hey I'd like to speak for a second. She immediately dismissed me and said she did not want to talk to me. At this point I literally do start having an anxiety attack and my heart is racing very fast. I kept my tone very calm and maintained courtesy, but I also got very excited internally and said "why would you say something like that to my friend when I went to the bathroom?!" 

She gave me little to no response and just said 'she's seen me talk to women before' (?!) and that she didn't trust me basically. My jaw drops at hearing this because I am very courteous and respectful in all my relations and I anxiously asked her to clarify, but to no avail...

She expressed she felt she had done nothing wrong in saying this to my friend when I walked away. At this point I feel so hurt and so confused and betrayed- I leave shortly after a very short and fruitless exchange with the worker with my heart in my stomach and my anxiety flying sky high.

A few days later I spoke with 2 of the owners there and explained the situation to them and they had absolutely NOTHING to say nor any goal to rectify the situation!! They didn't seem to care in the least and even went so far as to say they were going to 'side with their worker' regardless of the situations specifics and they asked me to not come back while she works there!! At this point I'm offended beyond belief and astounded at the ridiculous judgment call of their owners.

I quit going to blueprint at this point, but the story doesn't entirely end here. Fast forward like a year, maybe 1.5 years, and I walk back in the same store attempting to see if we can clarify and make amends but I don't even get to that point as I can see as soon as I walk in that the same woman who mischievously whispered to my friend was behind the counter and had been promoted to manager! She noticed me immediately and said I wasn't welcome there! It was embarrassing and another big anxiety explosion for me... 

I was hoping the owners would have corrected this, and I even waited a year after the last altercation hoping that one of them would speak with me or offer some clarification since we lived in the same neighborhood and would cross paths now and then. But they've never said a word to me since...

The fact that their owners deemed this appropriate, and felt it was wise to promote this individual, and also felt that making amends with me on a human level wasn't necessary has left a horribly bad taste in my mouth regarding blueprint coffee and I encourage anyone who takes sexism seriously to quit supporting them.
label: negative

text:
Right across from our hotel and we were starving after a long day of checking out the city. Reviews were decent so we wanted to try. Ordered the Rotolo (pic looks cool on website), Escarole Caesar, and a large Margarita pie.  Server asked if we wanted anchovies on the salad, and even though my daughter ordered it, I said sure. I love a couple of anchovies on a Caesar salad, so I thought if I snuck a bite, bonus for me. Server came back - no Rotolo.  Ok - so we ordered the breadsticks.  Breadsticks and salad arrived.  Breadsticks - no dipping sauce, kind of a disappointment - pretty good flavor from obviously their dough, but nothing more than Olive Garden really.  Caesar salad - wildly underdressed, so we asked for more dressing.  More dressing added, and completely overloaded with anchovies. Again, I love them, but so anchovy forward that if you like a good Caesar salad, this was nothing like that. It was kind of disgusting.  I am pretty sure that Caesar Salad is not native to Italy.  This should flat out be removed from their menu unless they change the recipe.  Lemon or salt would go miles to help this.  We got the "metro" size pizza as we were hungry; presentation 10/10, price 2/10 (I think it was $28 for a marginally sized flatbread), crust 10/10 (great chew and char), sauce 2/10 (prob San Marzano, but salt and olive oil were nonexistent), cheese 1/10 (almost non existent), and a handful of fresh basil thrown around on the flatbread.  
Service was pretty much as nonexistent as the flavor on the pie - we had to flag someone down if we wanted something.  I wouldn't be inclined to write this review if one person asked how our food was - no one did.  Seemed like a healthy takeout business which is good for them.  However, if you're trying to pick back up, customer service goes a long way.
Service - 1/10
Food  - 3/10
Overall - 3/10
Chance of returning - 0%
label: negative

All of the food was great and I even had a breakfast margarita! I was having a difficult time choosing an Eggs Benedict when I noticed the Peacemaker. You could get one each of two different benedicts, so I couldn't pass that up.

There is a lot of good food in New Orleans, but I can see why this is on everyone's list of must eat at places

text:
Went to the Woman's Exchange today for the first time in 30 years.
1.  Upon entering noticed the tables were at the most 2 feet apart.  They were trying to truly maximize the dining space they had.
2.  The lady that sat us was a " Volunteer" so she sat you at a table and that I believe is all she verbalized she was allowed to do.  Trust me she made this known to all of the diners.  She DEFINITELY DID NOT take orders, didn't know the soup of the day at all and did not offer to go to the kitchen 10 feet away to ask in the kitchen about soup for anyone.  I do think I saw her clear a table.  She was good at making herself look busy as we were there during lunch rush.  By all means I didn't see her volunteer to do anything more than " VOLUNTEER ".  If you are at a philanthropic organization one would believe you to have a personality that would be wanting to help others when they are very busy. The poor waitress was working her tushy off!!!!
3.  The poor waitress was so busy it was at least 15 minutes before she had a chance to get our order.  The " Volunteer" could have simply got our order and give it to the kitchen but the " Volunteer " made it clear to everyone she was a " VOLUNTEER ".  I'm not quite sure what is considered VOLUNTEERING or not.  This waitress was taking care of the ENTIRE room of customers which was easily 15 tables of customers.  I thought that the restaurant was probably breaking some type of OSHA law by having this poor woman taking care of ALL the tables.  I hope the diners left her nice tips because she was working hard.  It was really sad to watch!
4.  Women behind the counter where to go orders were being taken.  One women was taking orders and the other I think 2 were standing around looking busy.  They should have taken 10 steps back to the restaurant area to learn what busy is.
5.  I will say the epic salad was still very delicious.!  So, the cooks in the back get one star and I will give the poor waitress a star.  As the restaurant is rated as a whole they lost  my 3 stars.    The salad  was served with an awful English muffin that was cold with a butter Pat.  I know the history of the Woman's Exchange and this today was a pathetic visual of helping others in need.  The poor waitress was working without any help from people that I'm sure we're on the "special people board"to help for scholarships happen and assist less fortunate people.  I am really trying to use proper etiquette in this review and hold back on inappropriate language.  I rarely write reviews but I was admonished at what I saw in this place called a restaurant especially one with a philanthropic history at its heart!Chopped Salad

text:
Restaurant Week review-

Awesome service on this busy, Wednesday night. When we arrived for our 8:00PM reservation, the host informed us that it will be a 10-15 minute wait. We were able to go upstairs to the bar and enjoy an aperol spritz. Once we got seated, we chose our 4 courses! My husband and I picked different items off the menu, so that we could share - highly recommend! For our first course, we shared the meat and cheese platters. When we combined the two, it was like you had a very fancy, complete charcuterie board. My favorite, to my surprise, was the bologna! For our second course, we chose the arancini and the meatballs. The meatballs were something to write home about... authentic, moist, delicious! The sauce on both were very flavorful. For our third course, we shared the gnocchi and pappardelle. The gnocchi had a square of gelato that gave the gnocchi a slightly sweet, and cold burst to the mushroom sauce. If I had to choose, the pappardelle would be ordered again. And for our last course, gelato! How could you not? Get the stracciatella... you will not be disappointed! Thanks for a great evening.
label: positive

text:
If you have ever done a candle making experience before this place will be a HUGE disappointment. 

From the rude staff to the inaccurate website description this was all around a pretty disappointing experience. 

From the time we walked in the staff looked like we were bothering them patronizing the business. After getting checked in we were lead to a table and then just left there to figure it out. When trying to ask questions the staff literally just ignored us and walked away. This happened multiple times by different staff members who when I tried to speak to them literally walked away and disappeared in the back. The place wasn't even busy. The tables were more than half empty. 

We brought wine but were never offered any cups. No water. No one was even working the "coffee bar". 

At the table there are paper instructions on how to make your candle and you are pretty much just left to figure everything out yourself. 

Online they say they have 40 different fragrances. That's a huge lie. There were maybe a dozen to choose from unless they were hidden elsewhere but none of the staff let me ask any questions. There were a good amount of vessels to choose from but if I'm making a candle I'm a bit more interested in the scent than the container so that wasn't super impressive. 

Once you figure out what you need to do the process literally takes like 10 mins and at the end is the only time the staff will interact with you as they immediately take your supplies and try and rush you out. 

Also you don't get to leave with your candle. It takes 3 hours to cool and you have to come back later to get it which is inconvenient. 

There's better candle making and BYOB experiences in Philly. Don't waste your money here.
label: negative

text:
A smoothie is a smoothie for the most part, yes? With that being said, this review has nothing to do with the taste and/or quality of my fruity elixir. It does, however, have everything to do with the charming young lady that works there. I have to believe that she is actually going out of her way to be rude! From the time I had to peer my head around the corner to see if anyone was working there, to the time she literally threw a straw at me. Her commitment to nastiness was unflappable. 
Here's where I may lose a few of you... I found myself thinking about little miss sunshine for the better part of the day. I couldn't help thinking that I just caught her at a bad time. Like maybe she had just been told a beloved cat died or something? So the next day I put on my most chipper attitude and I found myself standing in front of the again abandoned kiosk. After craning my neck into the back room, out came my favorite employee with all the zeal of a wet napkin. We'll, how did it go this day? Let's just say, my over-the-top cheerfulness was met with such disdain it made me long for the viper who made my smoothie the previous day. Yikes! As I walked away I couldn't help thinking how lucky we are that she doesn't work at the ear piercing kiosk.
label: negative

text:
Roco's aka SB Roasting Co ratings of quality craft coffee are 80 out of about 90 points quality in coffee.  That is night and day better than most coffee house chains that at best might be 50 out of 90.  So that's actually very good.

We had been on auto ship-bill 5 LB bags for years and so far this year have shipped the wrong product twice which is why they have dropped from 4-5 stars to a 1 star review.  Having to tape up packages, print out labels and take them to UPS two times in a row to return them is a waste of time and energy.  Will give it one last try.  If they get it wrong 3 times in a row will stop doing business with them.

From what I've recently discovered there are 90 point craft coffee makers out there that cost a little bit more for higher quality online that auto ship-invoice several times a year.  They're sending me a sample to try.  

Great personal service and if it turns out to be better will go with this company and cancel with ROCO.  After years of being a loyal customer disappointed and will update once I try another supplier.  A company has to look in the mirror when they drive customers away to seek out better and more reliable coffee companies.

text:
I remember seeing this Vegan Ice Cream shop posted somewhere and have been meaning to stop by. Finally..The day arrived :) I was so excited. 
Coco N'Ice is a Vegan Ice Cream shop located in Tampa. Their slogan; "The Nutrition with a Good Conscience". In speaking with the owner herself, just because it's vegan it doesn't have to taste different. They offer delicious homemade ice cream flavors free of sugar & additives, Smoothies, Plant based shakes, Bliss balls, Açaí Bowls amongst so much more. Her shop is super cute, quaint and very clean. 
She allowed me to taste most of her flavors available. I chose Pistachio in a Charcoal flavored waffle cone and also brought home a delicious Tiramisu cake and few Bliss balls. 
They also offer a Loyalty Card for frequent customers and Catering. 
I can't wait to go back and visit when I'm the area.

text:
If you have 35-40 minutes to spare in order to wait for a cup of coffee then this is a nice backup option to your homemade cup of coffee. If you don't have that much time, I would steer clear. I literally, waited 25 minutes for an online coffee order that I never received and when I simply asked where I was in line to receive my coffee I was told it was like "mechanics" and they couldn't possibly determine that. Huh? Not exactly sure what that means. It's real simple to say, "you have five or ten people in front of you. So basically, my order was just sitting there in limbo as they continued to take more orders. I just wanted a cup of coffee and honestly didn't mind waiting a little bit for it. I just didn't have 40 minutes of time to spare for just one cup of coffee. I am a full time parent and owner of three separate businesses. People like me need to prioritize our time effectively. 

However, I told the guy working the counter that I wanted a refund because I didn't want to wait if it was going to be another 25 minutes. I just didn't have that kind of time. Especially, since we all had no idea how long it was going to take. He literally refused my refund and said that I need to have "patience." So not only did he not have my coffee ready after nearly thirty minutes, he refused to refund my money like he was some God and expected his peasant child to stand there and wait as she was told. So if my coffee isn't ready and you don't know when it will be ready, why can't I have my money back? Seems like the next logical step in this hourly servers "god complex" problem. Yes? 

And let's be honest, I think waiting 25 minutes for one cup of coffee is patience. Especially, considering I was merely asking for an eta. Which shouldn't be that hard to calculate. It also required a lot of patience for dealing with his shitty attitude in a calm manner while simultaneously being robbed for my five bucks. 

I know a lot of people want to try and control the actions and behaviors of other people by purposefully withholding something that is rightfully theirs or intentionally trying to punish them because they didn't fall in line or accept a lame ass, lazy response from someone that is expected to be knowledgeable and professional. Unfortunately, it seems narcissistic and misogynistic behavior is becoming more common. With that being said, contrary to what this hourly server may believe, I actually have the right to ask for a refund when the wait time is unusually longer than expected. I was polite in my request. It was the server that became unhinged when I didn't accept his half-assed answer to my question. He was Incredibly rude and he literally stole my five dollars. However, with his lazy attitude and their dysfunctional system, this is no surprise to me. 

They could care less if they lose one or two long term customers now. It's no big deal to them. It's nice that you can now treat people with such disrespect when they were just asking a simple question regarding time management. Terrible business morals. You should get rid of your To- Go option, if you expect people to wait 40 minutes for a cup of coffee. Or at least have the common courtesy to refund their money when they have determined that they don't have that much time to wait for it. 

I also have the screen shot notifications of the time I placed the order, to the time I was notified to pick up my order. If you would like me to provide this in order to prove that my statement is true, that can certainly be arranged.

Your hourly server has a solid disrespect for other people's time and is incredibly unprofessional. Not too mention a criminal for stealing my money and refusing to refund me for a product that I never received. He is not a God and his coffee is not God like quality. I will never step foot in your establishment again and I will never speak fondly of your establishment again.

You leave an open opportunity for people to give you bad reviews when you don't have a proper time management system put in place and you haven't trained your hourly staff members to properly respect your patrons time. I understand you're a lot more popular than you were a few years ago but that's still no excuse. You will garner more negative reviews if you don't address this problem. That's a fact.
label: negative

text:
Had dinner here last night. If you are looking for underwhelming Italian food at a reasonable price, then this is the place for you.  I started out with Lobster Bisque soup which I thought was way too thick.  I'm an East Coast transplant so I know my seafood dishes.  Lobster Bisque is meant to be more of a delicate soup with a lot of rich flavor.  This soup just did not cut it.  My next course was the Caprese Salad. The tomatoes were underripe and had no flavor.  If you can't get flavorful tomatoes to use  in a tomato salad, you shouldn't serve this salad at all.  Using heirloom tomatoes would have greatly improved the taste of this salad; instead, all the flavor came from their balsamic glaze and the basil which was the topping on the tomatoes and the cheese.  My next course was the Pork Scallopine with mushrooms and a balsamic creamy sauce.  What a surprise when it arrived as two large chewy pork chops which were pounded not so thin.  The typical Pork Scallopine or the Pork Scallopine I have always had in an Italian Restaurant, and how I prepare it at home,  has been made with the tenderloin of pork which is pounded very thin to create a tender, melt in your mouth feel from the pork.  If there are going to serve you a somewhat pounded pork chop, there need to describe it as such.  I asked the owner's wife what cut of pork they used and she told me it was not a pork chop and the server asked the kitchen and she told me it was a pork chop???

I had to give them 2 stars for the service we received and the fact that they exchanged my meal for something else.  The replacement was the Lamb Lollipops which were overcooked and strangely served with waffle fries.  They seem to be a bit confused about what they are serving as Italian food--  waffle fries did not seem to compliment the lamb at all.

I was so hopeful when we found out another Italian Restaurant opened in town since we seem to lack in number in Italian Restaurants compared to the other ethnic ones we have here in Tucson.

I am sorry to say we will not be returning for another disappointing dinner here.
label: negative

text: The food here is amazing, the most I've enjoyed breakfast in a long time. The crawfish omelette was amazing and so flavorful and tasted so fresh and healthy, who knew eggs could be that good?! As for the beignets, I tried beignets both here and cafe du monde and they were hands down better here...lighter, fluffier, less greasy, and more powdered sugar, I'm talking not even close. I also liked the environment much more, the jazz music playing inside and the cute cafe vibes all around, it really seemed to sum up the feeling of the city. So delicious and I would highly recommend it

text:
Finally! Mochi donuts in STL Thank you Pokedoke for bringing these amazing chewy donuts to us

Mochi donuts were originated from from Japan and is one of the star product at Mister Donut in Taiwan. It was super popular since about at least 10 years ago in Taiwan. I never seen these being sold here in the US until recent years. It's becoming a new hit in cities with more Asian populations. I am so super excited that we can finally get them here in St. Louis! 

If you never tried, they are a hybrid of cake donuts and mochi, with a dense and chewy texture. Usually a sugar/flavored glazed will be applied on one side of it. This week, they have Strawberry and Black Sesame and I love them both!!! Not quite as chewy as I would hope but it definitely meets my standard and every bite is so joyful

They have had Matcha, Taro, Cookies and Cream and powdered sugar flavors before. I think only the Delmar and Kirkwood locations have it right now. And they said you can contacted them with few days notice to customize the flavor you like for your event! I will definitely try to get all the flavors in the future

Pull into the parking lot and first thought was it sure smelled good, which I hoped meant good things for a restaurant that was recommended to me.  Seating was quick, restaurant was about 1/2 or better full.
Then I ordered.  Combo plate, ribs and brisket.  With beans and potato salad, and a Dr. Pepper.  Was asked if I wanted mild, medium, or hot BBQ sauce. 
 Never been faced with that choice before (note to self and others:  ask for sauce on side, you'll see why in a minute).  Waitress was pleasant enough.   Waited.  Waited some more.
Meal comes.
The hottest (warmest!) thing on the plate was the beans.  Ribs on top of brisket.  All pretty heavily coated in sauce.  Ribs tasted ok  (although I wouldn't call them championship quality).  But they were cool.  Definitely not hot.  Meat was so cool the sauce had the look of "I've been on here a while."  If you know BBQ you know what I mean.  What I found particularly mystifying was the 4th rib.  You know the end of the rack that is usually thinner than the rest?  And turns into a chunk of slag unless you really know how to cook ribs?  Yeah, they served me that.  A solid chunk of slag (and cool).
The brisket.  Ah, the brisket.  It tasted ok.....but was cooler than the ribs.  I really think if the brisket was warm, and had the sauce on the side, it might have been pretty dang good.  But, as it were, it was bleh.
The potato salad was your typical mass-produced, over mustardized (which is hard for me to say because I like mustard) potato cream like you'd find in a grocery store deli...in other words, bleh.
The beans, as I mentioned earlier, were the warmest thing served to me.  And they too were just ho-hum.  Kind of a weird flavor, actually.
Now I'll admit that I am real particular about how I like my beans and potato salad.  This definitely wasn't it.
You may be asking why I didn't say something to the waitress about the quality of the meal I was served....well, I would have, if I had gotten more than a fly-by "everything ok?" while I was eating.  I would think she would have noticed (and asked) that I didn't eat much when she dropped the check and scooped the plate.  But no.
So, $30 later (including tip) I left, very unsatisfied and still hungry.
Would I give them another chance? Sure, the brisket had potential.  I'd try it again just for that.
Otherwise, not so much.
label: negative

text:
I have to say, this is one touristy place that is 100% necessary and worth it in New Orleans.  I came during a recent trip on a weekend and there was a healthy line that moved quick.  Donut type pastries are one of my favorites so I was looking forward to them a lot. 

One order of beignets here is $3.40, and each order comes with 3 of them.  They come in a bag that has powdered sugar in them.  I also got the cafe au lait (medium is $3.50)  which seems like their most popular coffee choice.  Total came out to $7.50.  This place is cash only FYI.

The beignets were so perfect and delicious.  I guess they're so popular that they're always fresh and hot, at least at this location.  I almost had to slap someone when I bit into them!  So warm and doughy inside and the powdered sugar adds a delectable sweetness to it.  It was mind blowing and I couldn't get over how banging they were.  

The coffee was good too; it had a subtle sweetness to it and melted the beignets down really nicely.  It's nice and smooth and is a great compliment to the donuts.  I actually think the coffee is overpriced though; it's more expensive than an order of beignets by themselves.  

I think this was the best food experience I had in New Orleans.  It was so good that I came back again the next day for another round.  Definitely recommend it to everyone.
label: positive

text:
I was there for a planned 2 night complimentary stay on 1/09/22 &1/10/22.  I gave them about 9 hours of play on Sun & again on Mon.  On Monday afternoon, the dealer & then the supervisor threatened me with expulsion from the pai gow poker table because I made some mistakes setting my hand.  I pointed out that the mistakes were mine & I did not ask them to return my lost money, I accepted my losses without any complaint.  But I continued to be threatened that I would be asked to leave the table.  So after about the 5th threat from the dealer, she accomplished her apparent goal, I voluntarily left the table (& the casino), then checked out of the hotel a day early (with a free room).  Having made hundreds of casino visits over the years in several states, I have never before been treated rudely by a dealer or a supervisor.  This was the second rude dealer I have encountered at HR Tampa.  I returned to the table after cashing my chips to point out to the dealer that I was far more polite to her than she was to me.  I said nothing when she made 5 or 6 dealing mistakes over the course of 6 hours, including neglecting to pay a bonus hand for a full house, and for neglecting to deal all 7 hands another time. Perhaps I should have recommended to the supervisor that she be expelled from the table & replaced with a more competent dealer.

My suggestion is rather than spend any money for their hotel (over $500 per night), you would be better off using that money to purchase a plane ticket & hotel room at a Vegas casino.  It will likely be less expensive & you will be in an atmosphere where your patronage is appreciated.  This was far & away the worst casino experience I have ever seen.

If it wasn't for the great service I received in the hotel & the restaurant, my 1 star rating would have been lower.  My rating for the casino is below zero stars; the place owes me 3 stars.

Bottom line, church bingo is a better option.
label: negative

text: I don't have high expectation but my gf was hungry at the time. I got the korean BBQ wings and my gf got the Bun Bo Hue. We also ordered drinks. I have high standards when it comes to BBH. They are just overly breaded wings inconsistantly drizzled in some type of korean bbq sauce. I already had hesitations since this place is known more for the drinks and desserts more than food. What really turned me off was the service and the atmosphere. I understand this place is new and they are probably understaffed but a lot of things can be changed. Very loud... blaring TV with screaming children is typical for a place like this, but its the also the very loud blenders that make it hard to have any conversations. The owners need to understand that if its a small restaurant than please contain the noise levels. Finally, the unprofessional rude attitude. We had to order at the front desk, pay, take a number and sit. We ate what we can.... and sat and waited without any drinks that we ordered. They gave us a number to bring our food, but they DO NOT bring the drinks. You have to get the drinks yourselves at the front desk when they are done making it!!! We sat their not knowing this "policy" and when we finally asked about our drinks. They told us that it was done. Sitting at the front for us to get. Not only that... its been so long that my mango shush is more like a mango juice. Lol. I have never ate anywhere, even a self service atmosphere, where your food was brought out without any drinks. And when I asked, they said it was our job to monitor our drinks. To get it ourselves... I appreciate that they used actual fruit and not powder to make drinks, but overall the experience was terrible. For anyone thinking about going here, order drinks to go and don't bother getting anything to eat. You might just be doing them a favor.

text:
Busy cafe. Limited seatings during peak times. Difficult to social distance but at least cafe had the doors open. No mask enforced at all. We were in line and of 25 people in front of us, only 3-4 were wearing masks. Everyone were standing right next to each other. A few people were coughing and sneezing. I was pretty nervous. 

After a long wait, we ordered. They check vaccine cards before you order. We also snagged a table before ordering since it'll be difficult to find an open table. 

We ordered a latte and some beignets. The latte was ok, and the beignets were good but doughy too, not like the flaky soft pillowy ones we've had at a different cafe in the past. 

There were only 2 unisex bathrooms so there was a line. Bathrooms were decent.

There was a utensils and napkins station so you actually have to go get your own. I find this inconvenient especially when it's super busy and you have to fight your way through crowds just to get some napkins.

Not sure if I'd return. Maybe during a less busy time, as the beignets were decent.

The dip plate was really good also and had decent size portions for two people. I had lamb kebabs for my entrée and they were delicious, even though I did order the beef kebabs lol. My boyfriend had the Ful Mudammas and really didn't like it. The service was OK the waitress was a little bit weird towards us throughout our meal. text: Pretty disappointing. I've been wanting to eat here for several years based on what I thought was a great reputation in town.  I don't get over to the Mesa much but when I saw a coupon in a mailed flyer a couple months ago, I clipped it.  It offered a free entree with purchase of one entree and two drinks. Even with a discount of $12, the bill was $53 for lunch (before tip) -- I can eat a great dinner out for less than this -- so I was expecting a very delicious meal.  Both my companion and I felt the food was vastly overpriced for the quality, flavor and even presentation. The quantity was generous enough but the items we ordered (empanadas and the mushroom burger) were both rather bland.  The "burger" was served on a doughy, barely cooked pita-like bread rather than on an appropriate item to hold a messy burger in place like a brioche bun.  It was completely falling apart after a few bites... which would have been okay to eat with a fork except it was just not flavorful or complex given the list of ingredients. The most interesting item was the ketchup made from beets.  Also the side fries weren't thoroughly cooked except for the smallest ones.  Definitely not a $24 item! The empanadas were somewhat better but again, not a 'wow' and not even a 'hey, really tasty.' Just bland and again, not worth $24.  The half-off discount didn't even cover the $13 cost of a chai tea and lemonade (that was mostly ice of course).  All in all, very disappointing food and therefore completely overpriced.  We took the food to go so I cannot really comment on the service. When I read another lengthy review (12/5/21), I now see that the prices have been jacked and the quality has been wavering. I totally understand the precarious state of the restaurant business right now during covid but I cannot recommend this restaurant at this time.
label: negative

text:
As a Queens girl, I was so excited to go to the Mamajuana in Tampa hoping for the same awesomeness from back home but boy was I wrong. The restaurant is beautiful, however that's about it. I ordered calamari (yes I know...who orders that at a Spanish restaurant) as an appetizer and they looked more like onion rings because they were so greasy and fat and were way too chewy and had zero flavor. Our drinks (party of 8) were terrible. Either it tasted like rubbing alcohol or was too watered down to taste anything. 

My friend ordered the chicharrón the pollo app and it definitely did not taste the way it should and it was also way too soft, something just wasn't right. Chicharrón is supposed to be crunchy and tasty and as soon as you bite into it, it takes you back to the motherland...but this took me to a dark alley I never want to walk through. My husbands rice was undercooked which he couldn't even eat and he barely ate the rest of his meal because it just didn't have any taste. I ordered a chicken mofongo with plátano, not the yuca one, and man was it underwhelming. I took three bites and left the rest.  My friend ordered a salad and they said they were out of romaine so the waitress said we have mixed greens instead, friend says yes and receives a plate of spinach only (the only mixing being done was with the DJ). 

Speaking of which, nice touch with the live band, however you couldn't even hear the singers because the base sounded so bad and loud which drowned them out. The option of being able to go to the back lounge portion was nice for a change once you're done not stuffing your face. 

Unfortunately I would definitely not be returning. Not one of us fully ate their meals or even half way, the prices are outrageous (I've eaten better food for way less and didn't have to drive 45 minutes), they just need to do better. If they don't pick up the pace a lot of people are going to be out of a job. Take notes from your fellow NY branches and get some act right in you, otherwise the doors will be closing sooner than you know. Wishing y'all the best but some major changes need to happen.
label: negative

text:
We walked into the cafe in the direction of the counter and as we stood there, there were two people behind the counter - young man and older woman. Neither really spoke to us for few minutes as we awkwardly stood in pretty tight enclosed space. At some point the woman standing there writing in her notepad looked up and asked if we were being helped which we obviously weren't as she and the other guy that could've spoken to us. She asked for our vaccine cards before anything (which isn't a problem until a bit later) and then told us there were counter seats in the back or we can just wait for one of the tables to be cleaned and we can sit there. We opted to wait and we're seated shortly after. While we waited for someone to take our order, about two more crowds walked in and were politely greeted immediately and told them someone would be with them in one minute. Right after, another group walked in and after being greeted, they were advised to have a seat and would be asked to show their cards when someone came to the table.

My partner and I immediately noticed a large difference in customer care between us and the groups following us; I'm not one to say this often but it definitely felt like it was because we're are people of color.
We decided not to eat there and didn't even bother to try their food based on their service. I'm a big fan of small cafe's but this was disrespectful for my own taste - Philly has much better places!

text:
i made and paid for my hotel reservation in late November. first of all, the central reservation personnel, though polite, are not very qualified. they will rarely transfer you to the hotel, even when you have an issue. i wanted to speak with hotel management and they told me they were not allowed to transfer me to hotel management. i made reservations at Council Oaks for a dinner birthday celebration for my moms 85th birthday. it was to be a party of 10. i specifically asked if seperate checks were ok and was told they were. when i called back and spoke to Council Oak directly closer to my arrival, they told me only 2 tickets were allowed for large parties and they did not even show the cake i had ordered months prior for $75 dollars. so when i got to the hotel on Jan. 5th i called and spoke to front desk manager Chris, very nice person, to advise him we decided not to eat at Council Oaks but at the HardRock Cafe instead. Chris could not even find a reservation for my party of 10.  just imagine if we had showed up for dinner at Council Oaks and there was no reservation, how embarrassing would that have been. Chris apologized and said he would look into what happened and thought perhaps the reservation was make at the Council Oaks at Seminole Hard 
Rock in Ft Lauderdale. another slip up of the central reservation personnel. so we had dinner at HardRock Cafe which was adequate. our server LD was not very good. we had 2 filet mignon dinners ordered one meduim and one medium rare. well the food runner gave the wrong plates to the wrong people and before we determined the problem it was too late and one of the people had begun eating the food. LD should have had them cut into the steaks to make sure they received the proper steaks, but half the time we couldnt even find LD our server. other than these issues, everyone and everything was fine. but you expect a 4 star hotel to be more polished. i certainly would review the central reservation personnel and get them all on the same page.

text: Mediocre food, and vegetarians and others beware! Walked by here on a weekend and lots of people there so we decided to give it a try on a weekday. Not crowded at all. I am a vegetarian and didn't see a lot on the menu for me so I ordered a grilled cheese with tomato, and NO BACON. My daughter ordered the donuts French toast, which sounded so good from the description. Our food was delivered by a different waitress and when I saw the pile of bacon on my sandwich I told her I was a vegetarian and ordered it without bacon. She was very nice about and said she was a vegetarian too and understood. About 2 minutes later my sandwich reappeared and our original waitress apologized and admitted it was her fault, and she put it in wrong. At first glance it looked ok but on further inspection, a few pieces of bacon were left behind, as apparently they thought it was acceptable to simply remove the greasy meat from my sandwich. I was disgusted that the kitchen/waitress would think this was appropriate. I specifically told both waitresses that I was a vegetarian. The donut French toast was not appealing either. It was dry and was served with small amount of a semi-congealed sauce  on top. The waitress finally came back and I told her about the problem with my sandwich. She made no apologies and never came back out. After 10 minutes I finally went in and spoke to the manager, who also made no apologies, but offered to make me something else.  I explained that they completely lost my trust and I didn't want anything else. I told them to just comp our food and be done with it. Absolutely unacceptable!!! No excuses!

text:
I would give this restaurant zero stars if I could. The food was served rotten, cold, and the service was terrible. When we confronted the owner she was unapologetic and rude. We can hear the chef swearing in the kitchen behind us. The service was slow. We had two diabetics in our party and asked for Diet Coke and they brought us regular coke (very dangerous). Half the menu wasn't available. No silver wear until after the food was served. The food was half the portions than usual for the same price. All of the dishes that were supposed to be hot were cold. Service was atrocious. One of the salads was served with rotten lettuce. Little to no dressing on all of the salads. Only one of the terrible dishes was taken off the ticket where I would argue they all should have been.
I don't write reviews, but this was too bad not to write about. My family is Italian and used to love this restaurant as we have been going here for years, but regrettably this was our last time. Kitchen nightmares would have been very apt for this restaurant. #GordonRamsayComeHelp

text:
We had reservations here for Christmas Day Reveillon Dinner. It was supposed to be a 4 course meal but ended being only 3 for us because after 2 plus hours we requested the check.

The servers were trying their best and fielding several complaints from tables around us. It was obvious there was some back of the house issues and I felt bad for the servers that had to take the brunt of the blame. They were so overwhelmed that nobody even batted an eye at our request for a check before the end of meal or gave thought to apologies or make it rights. In my opinion, management failed the servers and left them out to dry. 

Advertised menu items were not available. We were only served one drink and never asked if we would like another. Honestly it got to the point that it was amusing instead of infuriating. 

What food we did eat was good. Understandable that it was Christmas and may have been dealing with staffing shortages. There appeared to be plenty of staff just not utilized in best way possible.

text:
Went to the downtown Tucson location in October 2021. The atmosphere is cool but I really had a negative experience due to poor customer service. I ordered a cappuccino and when it was ready, I noticed that it came in a paper cup. I told the barista that I had wanted it for here but the barista was just like 'well here is your coffee'. 

No apology, no offer to remake it or even just pour it into a regular cup (which would have been fine). Nothing. I have never had this level of poor service at a coffee shop before, which really took me back. The cashier hadn't originally asked me if I wanted my coffee to eat in or to go, and there were customers there with regular cups, so I didn't think I had to specify this.

The coffee was ok. It wasn't bad by any means, but not exceptional either. Given the bad experience I had with the folks who were working that day, I likely won't ever patronize them again.

Kara Lynn also supports local women-owned businesses. I HIGHLY recommend Kara Lynn's Kitchen. Eat there, and you'll discover why I'm posting this stellar review.

text:
Meh-Underwhelmed although my waiter was super attentive and great
Ambiance uninventive cliched and aged getting close to faded ...
Very unclear what the fuss is about  this place to me just a super average french aspiring  bistro you find anywhere ...
First although place totally empty after 3 seating attempts they kept seating me a black solo female diner in the bleachers - busy corridor aisle ,next to drafty window and restock settings,last next to cashier -WHY????
i have read about restaurants treating diners of a certain hue this way but this is first time in my 50 years  I actually experienced ...
Second food meh / started with escargots which were served like a weird soup in a bowl of strange mushroom liquid and mixed with bella mushrooms - hello for $15 you better give me all escargots with loads of butter /garlic /parsley ...
Second course steak frites steak was cooked perfect but frites frozen seeming average...
Star in the meal  was the tarte tatin which is one of the best I have had in a long time... 
drinks pricey but adequate 
overall would not recommend - this is not true french cuisine by any stretch  - have had loads better food in this part of philly and philly in general ...
Maybe they were better on their glory days but looks like the show is long over

text:
Stopped here with my better half for a late breakfast. Watching the menus a bit closer now that we are back in Weight Watchers after a COVID pause.

Close to us and recommended by our daughter, we were in the area and decided to stop by. Plenty of parking and easy to find. Lots of patio tables but a bit too chilly for me. Service was quick.

Staying away from all my favorites, I checked the point counts and decided on the Greek Omelette with spinach, tomatoes and Feta cheese, plus I added jalapeños. As a side I went for Sweet Potato Fries. Oh and unbuttered whole wheat toast (but I only ate 1/2 of a slice so most points were the fries. My wife went for, as usual, a Southwest Omelette, also adding jalapeños. I guess I have influenced her over the past 50 years!

Our food came quickly. My wife raved about her omelette and she had sliced tomatoes as a side. Plus she had a piece of marble toast with strawberry jam, to match my fries. My omelette was really good, my first but definitely not my last. The winner of the meal was definitely the Sweet Potato Fries! Hot, crispy outside and soft inside. And unlike others I've had in past, nice big fries. Ummmm ummm, fantastic! 

Great meal, great service. Definitely a spot to come check out.
label: positive

text:
The last couple of times I've been here I haven't been impressed. Both times, I ordered a flavored lemonade, the last time, it was the pina Colada lemonade. There wasn't any flavored added, very bland. I'd say it was a one off, but I think they always forget or very stingy with the flavorings. It also takes forever to get the drink, very slow service. For the price you pay for a lemonade, you'd think it would be amazing and good service. 
I also wanted to try the flights since there are so many options but they don't offer them if you go through the drive thru. This is very disappointing since we are in a pandemic and all. There could at least be communication on the drive thru sign about what they refuse to serve. I'm disappointed and hope they improve.
label: negative
